name: session_start
description: Session startup workflow. Read state → diff DB → flag questions → report ready.

nodes.instruction: Read `{PROJECT_ROOT}/workspace/project_state.md`. Confirm project type from first line (`<!-- type: knowledge|project_management -->`). Set `{PROJECT_ID}` for this session. Summarize to user: 當前階段, 活躍任務 count, 未解問題 count, 資料庫現況. Then run: bash shared/tools/conda-python.sh shared/tools/kb.py project-state-index --project {PROJECT_ID}  (this writes projects/{PROJECT_ID}/workspace/.project_state.json — the sidecar that flag_open_questions and downstream nodes will read). Complete with: coordinator.py complete read_project_state --outputs '{"state_path":"projects/{PROJECT_ID}/workspace/project_state.md"}' --sidecar projects/{PROJECT_ID}/workspace/.project_state.json

nodes.description: Generate fresh project-scoped active_rules_summary, then Read it so the active D-NNN ruleset is in context for this session. Cost: ~3K tokens; benefit: prevents Tier-3 rewrites from drifting away from active decisions.
nodes.instruction:
(1) Run: bash shared/tools/conda-python.sh shared/tools/kb.py generate-summary --project {PROJECT_ID} --output projects/{PROJECT_ID}/workspace/.active_rules_summary.md  (2) Note the 'Sidecar: ...json' line printed to stdout — that is the authoritative count source.  (3) Use the Read tool on projects/{PROJECT_ID}/workspace/.active_rules_summary.md so the rules enter your context.  (4) Complete: coordinator.py complete load_active_context --sidecar projects/{PROJECT_ID}/workspace/.active_rules_summary.json

Do NOT count D-NNN tokens in the file — the validator reads active_decision_count directly from the sidecar. If you also pass --outputs, your active_decision_count must agree exactly with the sidecar's value.

nodes.description: If project has backlog.db (project_management type), run backlog.py summary and remind to show open P0/P1, blocked, and stale items. Skipped for knowledge-type projects.
nodes.instruction: Only if project type is 'project_management': run bash shared/tools/conda-python.sh shared/tools/backlog.py --project {PROJECT_ID} summary, then bash shared/tools/conda-python.sh shared/tools/backlog.py --project {PROJECT_ID} remind. Report open P0/P1, blocked, stale items. Skip entirely for 'knowledge' type projects.

nodes.description: Run kb_index.py validate to detect expired TTL, supersede inconsistencies, and semantic conflicts. Report any WARN/ERROR to user.
nodes.instruction: Run: bash shared/tools/conda-python.sh shared/tools/kb.py validate --warn-exit-zero. Report any ERROR or WARN entries to user. Do not block session on WARN-only results. ERROR entries require user acknowledgment.

nodes.description: Check DB for changes since last session (new tables, row counts)
nodes.instruction: If {PROJECT_ROOT}/workspace/db/*.db exists: run bash shared/tools/conda-python.sh shared/tools/db_schema.py show <db_path> --compact. Compare row counts with project_state.md 資料庫現況 block. Note any new tables or row count changes >10%.

nodes.description: Identify and flag open questions from project_state.md
nodes.instruction:
Read the .project_state.json sidecar produced in read_project_state. Its outputs.open_question_count and outputs.open_question_classification are AUTHORITATIVE — do NOT recount by hand. When open_question_classification is 'placeholder', 'empty', or 'missing', the count is 0 regardless of the .md file's body text (e.g. '（無）', '(none)', etc. are all normalised to 0).

For project_management projects, also run: bash shared/tools/conda-python.sh shared/tools/backlog.py --project {PROJECT_ID} list --status blocked.

Complete: coordinator.py complete flag_open_questions --outputs '{"open_question_count":N,"open_questions_summary":"..."}' --sidecar projects/{PROJECT_ID}/workspace/.project_state.json

(N must equal the sidecar count; summary must say '(none)' when N=0.)

required_outputs.description: summary of open questions or explicit none marker

nodes.description: Generate a project-scoped session context bundle that combines active decisions, recent learnings, and open questions for this session.
nodes.instruction:
(1) Run: bash shared/tools/conda-python.sh shared/tools/kb.py generate-session-context --project {PROJECT_ID} --output projects/{PROJECT_ID}/workspace/.session_context_bundle.md  (2) Note the 'Sidecar: ...' line from stdout.  (3) Read the generated bundle and surface its counts to the user.  (4) Complete: coordinator.py complete build_session_context --sidecar projects/{PROJECT_ID}/workspace/.session_context_bundle.json

If you pass --outputs with bundle_*_count fields, they must match the sidecar exactly (sidecar is authoritative).

required_outputs.description: path to generated session context bundle
number of active decisions in bundle
number of recent learnings in bundle
number of open questions in bundle

nodes.description: Report session ready status to user with context summary
nodes.instruction:
Report in 3-5 bullet points: project type + {PROJECT_ID}, 當前階段, active decision count, open question count, KB health status, DB status. If open questions exist, explicitly surface them as risk. End with: 就緒，等待指示。 Complete with {"ready_summary":"...","risk_status":"clear|open_questions|mixed","context_bundle_path":"projects/{PROJECT_ID}/workspace/.session_context_bundle.md","reported_open_question_count":Q}.

Validator constraints (must match exactly, else FAIL):
- reported_open_question_count MUST equal the sidecar count from flag_open_questions (the validator pulls this from .project_state.json automatically)
- context_bundle_path MUST equal build_session_context's sidecar bundle_path
- risk_status='clear'          requires count == 0
- risk_status='open_questions' requires count > 0
- risk_status='mixed'          allowed when there is an additional risk surface beyond open questions

required_outputs.description: bundle path echoed in ready report
open question count surfaced in ready report
